product.title: Canada Pooch Dog Cooling Vest - Evaporative Cooling Vest for Dogs with Breathable Mesh Material & Reflective Lining, Adjustable Dog Cooling Vest Great for Dogs 10 (9-11" Back Length), Aqua
feature_bullets: CHILL SEEKER - Do you want to bring your dog on an outdoor adventure but are worried about the heat? That’s why we came up with the Chill Seeker! Our cooling dog vest contains water retaining fabric and as the sun comes in contact with the held moisture, the water will evaporate. This ensures your dog will be kept cool on hot days; whether you’re on a bike ride or taking a fun beach day! Just add water, wring, put over your dog’s head, adjust the buckle, and you’re ready to cool down!

COMFORTABLE MESH LAYERS - When we set out to create our cooling dog harness we didn’t want to compromise on your dog’s comfort! The material inside the vest has breathable mesh air pockets to allow air circulation. The outer shell contains 100% Polyester Mesh, while the interior material contains a blend of 80% Rayon & 20% Polyester. Our material is also chemical coolant free & the outer coat has reflective lining, which will keep your dog in sight during the day or night!

GREAT FOR DOGS OF ALL SIZES - Our dog cooling coat is fully adjustable for dogs of any size. Our sizing chart will show the best matching method broken down by your dog’s breed or size. For back length, measure by the base of the neck to the base of the tail, & for larger dogs make sure to measure around the dog under their front legs for the full chest girth. The cool vest attaches through a buckle so if it takes a couple of tries to adjust it won’t be an issue!

UTILITY DOG WEAR WITH STYLE - The Chill Seeker comes in a variety of styles and colors because we didn’t stop at just functionality and comfort. We believe in having style with substance - so we created fun designs that we would wear ourselves!

A POSITIVE IMPACT ON DOGS - At Canada Pooch, we know there are other dog owners out there that care about function, quality, AND style. After 10 years of being in business, Canada Pooch has continued to service pet parents that don’t want to compromise on quality and function so you can make the most of your time with your pup!

local.title: The perfect cooling vest for summer!
local.text: The media could not be loaded. 28 lb frenchie- size 16 fit like a glove. I bought 6 vests from different vendors and I can say this was the favorite. Quality, durability, water retention, cooling effect, comfort, won’t slide off! My frenchie hates clothing but even he knew this was a good vest. Once you wet this it remains cool for a while. My dog no longer pants after short periods outside. This is a great product for summer. Also we have received so many compliments on the style. My advice is to make sure you are measuring the length correctly I bought a size up and had to return (it was too long) because frenchies always need more head-neck room. I went w the exact size and his chonky neck/head fit through the neck opening!
local.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 6, 2025

local.title: Great product
local.text: This really helps keep our dog cool. He is always hot and drives us nuts with constant panting. He is so much more comfortable with the vest.
local.date: Reviewed in the United States on December 13, 2025

local.title: It fit well.
local.text: I ordered according to the measurements chart and it was correct for my corgi. It was easy to put on and to adjust. I am not sure how well it kept her cool, however. She seemed to behave as if it was just another layer she didn't want or need. That's on her. But she is one dog that definitely needs cooling since she has a double coat. I will try it again with her but I am not sure how much she will enjoy it. She struggles with the heat. In the winter I can't keep her indoors. She absolutely adores the snow and cold.
local.date: Reviewed in the United States on July 6, 2025

local.title: Works great for doberman
local.text: I got a size 26 for my 11.5 month old doberman, Angus. He is 75% European, 95ish pounds, and his chest measures about 35" and his back is 26". If it's sunny out, he overheats if it's 70 degrees or more. The sizing of this item is so much better than most other similar items, because it's mostly just the back length. But anyone with a doberman knows how difficult it can be to get coats, harnesses, etc. because they have such a deep chest. We used this cooling vest for the first time today. It was about 84 degrees and full sun here in Michigan. I ran the vest under cold water in the sink, wrung it out, put it on, and headed out for a walk. At first he was a little weirded out about the straps around his back legs, but he was over it within a minute and never seemed to mind them after that. Typically, when it's even just 70 degrees and sunny, Angus starts to overheat within a quarter mile. Today we made it a full mile before he started panting, and that's with him off leash, running through ditches and fields, not just walking next to me. With his neck and head still exposed to the sun, he was a bit warm. I constantly checked under the vest and it was always nice and cool, even after a 2.5 mile walk. A few times I squeezed the chest part to get some water to run over his head and neck to cool him off a little. Without the vest, there was no way we could've walked as far as we did, so I'd say it's definitely worth it!
local.date: Reviewed in the United States on May 19, 2024

local.title: Must have in San Diego
local.text: As a mobile dog trainer living in sunny and dry North County, San Diego and who brings her dog with her every day, this is a must, especially during the summer. It helps her keep cool in the hot weather. I just pour water on it, make sure it soaks in, and slap it on her. Fits great, even with my deep chested pup.
local.date: Reviewed in the United States on September 24, 2025

local.title: Great protection for my large dog
local.text: Searched high and low for a protective shirt. My 85 lb pitbull has severe allergies and hair loss. This is super lightweight and slips over his head with ease and protects him. The 2 side buckles are easy to snap and unsnap. It takes minimal adjusting to get the right fit. The length is perfect, but I do wish the sides were a bit longer. I put his harness on over the shirt, which keeps it in place nicely. I'm so glad I don't have to put his front legs into this. He would not like that. This is easy on and off. For the hot days, I pour water on the shirt to cool him down. It doesn't weigh much even wet, and we can walk much further because he is happy and cool. Thank you!
local.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 25, 2022

local.title: Order much longer than you measure
local.text: So first, I measured my golden retriever as instructed here. I ordered the length measured, and it was far too short. So I sent it back and got the next larger size. That is also too short, but we had temps in the 90s, so I kept it. I wish it had a second strap or belt lower down on the body, as this falls off to the side with nothing securing the back end. I have been using one of my own belts on it, which helps, but this is definitely a design flaw.Also, my dog is 13 1/2. She has terminal cancer and a bad leg, so we walk about one mile. I soak the vest in cold water with ice in it, then barely wring it out. It helps cool her for that short walk in 75-85 degree heat, but I wouldn’t push it unless you take along extra ice water to re-wet, or have access to a mountain spring on your walk. Suffice it to say it’s better than nothing, but honestly it’s worth about $20.00.
local.date: Reviewed in the United States on May 29, 2022

local.title: Fits well
local.text: Very nice, lightweight, cooling vest. It’s beautifully my dog doesn’t mind it. It’s great for when we do fast cat in the summer.
local.date: Reviewed in the United States on April 8, 2025

global.text: Marvellous product. Keeps the dog cool even in the hottest day. Very pleased. Also fits perfectly.

global.title: Hole for Leash to attach to harness and cooling
global.text: There is a hole in top of cooling vest for a leash to fit through,was a question I had and couldnt find an answer, so here it is , I have a sheltie/border collie mix very active with a long back,deep chest and short legs which I have had troubles getting rain coats etc to fit all the way down her back , I bought a size 26" for cooling vest and this one even covers just over the start of tail YEA!! She is 26" from base of neck to start of tail.The leg elastics I will cut one side of each and bring one end from right side under her belly up to left side top and oppisite on other side and attach at top with a clip >> Elastic Cord Adjusters because she has long hair in underneath of her back legs and they will do nothing but matt up in fur,as for cooling I will have to let you know in furture because our hot humid 37 c weather just dropped to 16c with a nice breeze
